title: FilmPermitApplication
description: An application to the MOME Film Office for a film/photography location permit — the everyday production transaction. NET-NEW: there is NO public API for applying. Today an applicant applies only inside the login-walled 'MOME E-Apply' ASP.NET portal (nyceventpermits.nyc.gov/film) or via a Letter in Lieu of Permit by email. The Film Permits Open Data (tg4x-b46p) publishes the OUTPUT (issued permits) but never the intake. This schema serves both the read surface (an applicant's own applications and their status) and the write surface (apply for a permit).

applicationId.description: Server-assigned application number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource. Becomes the FilmPermit EventID when issued.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by the Film Office, not the applicant.

subCategoryName.description: Production sub-category (Feature, Episodic series, Commercial, Documentary, Student, ...).

locations.description: The street segments / held locations being requested (ScreenActivity to be granted).

parkingHeld.description: Free-text description of parking/street holds requested, if not itemized in locations.

insuranceOnFile.description: Whether the required certificate of insurance is on file (a prerequisite for issuance).

feeWaiverRequested.description: Whether a fee waiver is requested (evaluated case-by-case; base fee is $500 per 14-day period).

letterInLieu.description: Request a 'Letter in Lieu of Permit' instead of a full permit (exterior public property, low-impact shoots).

notes.description: Free-text notes to the Film Office.

issuedPermitEventId.description: EventID of the issued FilmPermit, once approved.
